reasoning:
The stock closed at $56.09 on December 19, 2025, which sets the threshold for this question. A critical factor is that Altria declared a $1.06 dividend with an ex-dividend date of December 26, 2025. 

For the first resolution date (Dec 28, assessing the Dec 26 close), the stock will instantly drop by ~$1.06 at the open on Dec 26. For its closing price to remain above $56.09, MO essentially must gain ~2% over the next 3 trading days prior to the ex-dividend date. Without an imminent catalyst, this is highly unlikely for a low-beta tobacco stock (~15% chance). 

For January 20, 2026, the stock has about one month to overcome the $1.06 dividend drag. Based on historical volatility (~5% monthly stdev) and expected drift, overcoming this ~2% disadvantage represents approximately a 0.3 standard deviation hurdle, correlating to a ~38% chance of being above the $56.09 threshold.

For March 21, 2026 (assessing the Mar 20 close), the time horizon expands to three months, making variance much higher. Under a random walk with slight upward drift, three months is typically enough to smooth out a single dividend drop, bringing the probability close to 50%. A small downside risk remains if Altria's Q1 ex-dividend date lands earlier than expected (historically it usually strikes between March 22-26), but it will most likely fall after March 20. Thus, probability rises to ~47%, reflecting increased uncertainty and normal market drift towards the mean over a longer horizon.
